fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to indiana, respiratory therapist compact indiana, indiana respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Indiana PLA — Respiratory Care Committee
board.mailing_address:
Indiana Professional Licensing Agency
402 W Washington Street, Room W072
Indianapolis, IN 46204

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Indiana.

steps.title: Create or Log In to MyLicense Indiana Account
steps.description: Access the MyLicense Indiana online services portal at mylicense.in.gov/eGov/ and either create a new user account or log in if you already have one.

steps.title: Start a New Application
steps.description: Once logged in to MyLicense Indiana, navigate to 'Apply for a new license'. Select 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' as the profession and choose the appropriate application type (e.g., 'RCP by Examination' or 'RCP by Endorsement').

steps.title: Complete the Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill out the online application completely within the MyLicense Indiana system, providing personal information, educational background, and details regarding your NBRC credentials.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload necessary documents through the MyLicense Indiana portal. This typically includes official transcripts from an accredited respiratory care program and proof of your NBRC credential (CRT/RRT).

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $50 directly through the MyLicense Indiana online payment system to complete your submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through your MyLicense Indiana account and promptly respond to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Indiana Respiratory Care Committee.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Indiana respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Indiana have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Indiana. To work in Indiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Indiana
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Indiana, you must use the MyLicense Indiana online services portal. You will create an account, complete the application, upload documents, and pay the required fees through this system.

official_links.registration_steps: To apply for a license online, you must first create a new user account or log in to an existing account through MyLicense Indiana. Once logged in, select 'Apply for a new license' to begin your application.
